summary: Iteration 9 (Bulk Delete cards) — both backend (9/9 pytest) and frontend e2e flows passed. Backend POST /api/cards/bulk-delete handles auth (401), validation (empty=400, >500=400, missing body=422), owner-scoped deletion, silent skip of non-existent/foreign ids, and correct deleted count. Frontend select-mode UX works end-to-end: enter/exit toggle, checkbox overlay, floating bulk-action-bar with live count, Select-all↔Clear label toggle, Delete button disabled at 0, window.confirm flow, toast 'Deleted N cards', auto-exit select mode, grid refresh. Regressions clean: lightbox suppressed in select mode, single DELETE /api/cards/{id} and GET /api/cards unaffected, no foreign-user data touched.

issues: Many buttons in one row (All-time, Import CSV, Export, Tax 8949, My Recap, Leaderboard, Select, Add Card) — OK at 1920px but could wrap tightly on md/lg; consider grouping actions into an overflow menu.
Recharts emits width/height(-1) warnings to console on initial mount — harmless but noisy; consider setting explicit minWidth/minHeight or aspect ratio on ResponsiveContainer.

critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx is ~590 lines — borderline; consider extracting the bulk-select bar and title-row action group into sub-components to keep file <500 lines.
onBulkDelete uses window.confirm — acceptable, but consistent with rest of app which also uses window.confirm for single delete, so parity is fine.
CollectionCard wrapperClass concatenation is getting long; ring-offset uses hardcoded #0A0A0A which matches bg but would break if theme changes.
Good: backend bulk_delete uses $in + user_id scoping in a single delete_many query (efficient + safe) and soft-deletes attached files via $in update_many.
Nice: non-existent ids are silently skipped and deleted_count returned — contract matches frontend toast.
test_credentials: demo@example.com / demo1234 (Pro). Auth login response key is 'token' (not 'access_token').
seed_data_creation: Created 3 TEST_bulk_ui_* throwaway cards for UI flow, deleted 2 via bulk-delete UI and 1 via API cleanup. Backend tests also create TEST_bulk_* cards and clean up via the bulk-delete / DELETE endpoints they exercise. Also created one throwaway user (test_bulk_<hex>@example.com) to verify cross-user isolation; their single test card was cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth login returns {token, user}, not access_token. Backend uses user-scoped delete_many. Demo seeded cards (LeBron RC, Giannis Select, etc.) were NOT touched. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_9.py; run via: pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_9.py -v.
